projects.title: Clarendon Street Two-Level Basement Excavation
projects.summary: Bulk excavation and retention for a two-level basement car park beneath a mixed-use development.
projects.description: GroundCore delivered detailed basement excavation for a two-level underground car park on Clarendon Street, including temporary propping, shotcrete walls and dewatering. Tight access and heritage facades required staged excavation and continuous monitoring to maintain adjoining building stability.

projects.title: Bourke Street CBD Basement Excavation
projects.summary: Deep basement excavation for a 3-level basement in Melbourne CBD with strict vibration limits.
projects.description: This CBD project involved excavation to 11 metres below street level for three basement levels. GroundCore managed a complex retention system of soldier piles and shotcrete, real-time vibration monitoring, and night-time spoil haulage to minimise CBD disruption.

projects.title: St Kilda Road Residential Basement
projects.summary: Basement excavation for a premium residential tower with constrained access on St Kilda Road.
projects.description: GroundCore completed detailed excavation and base slab preparation for a single-level basement beneath a high-end residential tower. Works included underpinning adjacent properties, managing high groundwater and coordinating with piling crews under a compressed program.

projects.title: Richmond Townhouse Basement Garages
projects.summary: Basement excavation for 12 townhouses with shared underground parking in Richmond.
projects.description: A cut-and-cover basement solution was delivered for a dense townhouse development. The team executed detailed excavation, temporary shoring along narrow laneways, and coordinated spoil removal through a single access point without disrupting neighbours.

projects.title: Box Hill Mixed-Use Basement
projects.summary: Two-level basement excavation for a mixed-use podium and tower in Box Hill activity centre.
projects.description: GroundCore undertook bulk and detailed excavation to support a two-level basement including ramp formation, lift pits and stormwater detention tanks. The project required careful staging around live retail tenancies and tram operations.

projects.title: Moonee Ponds Apartment Basement
projects.summary: Single-level basement excavation beneath a mid-rise apartment building in Moonee Ponds.
projects.description: The project scope covered bulk excavation, detailed trimming to founding level, and preparation for pile caps and lift pits. Traffic management on a constrained inner-suburban site was a key component of the delivery strategy.

projects.title: Werribee 48-Lot Residential Subdivision Earthworks
projects.summary: Bulk earthworks, trimming and lot preparation for a 48-lot greenfield subdivision.
projects.description: Scope included bulk cut-to-fill, topsoil stripping, road boxing, and detailed trimming for 48 residential lots. GroundCore also managed wet weather controls and liaison with surveyors to maintain lot levels.

projects.title: Logan 32-Lot Subdivision Earthworks & Retaining
projects.summary: Earthworks and retaining wall platforms for a sloping 32-lot subdivision in Logan.
projects.description: This project combined bulk earthworks with timber and concrete sleeper retaining walls to create benched house pads on a challenging sloping site. Works were delivered under staged releases to align with builder programs.

projects.title: Brisbane Riverfront Screw Piling for Luxury Home
projects.summary: Installation of screw piles up to 10m deep for a high-end riverfront dwelling.
projects.description: GroundCore installed 96 structural screw piles in soft alluvial soils adjacent to the Brisbane River. Piles were torque-monitored to verify capacity, and works were sequenced to coordinate with basement excavation under a tight residential program.

projects.title: Carlton Terrace House Extension Screw Piles
projects.summary: Residential screw piling for a two-storey rear extension in Carlton.
projects.description: Lightweight screw piles were installed for a two-storey rear extension behind a heritage terrace. Piles to 8.5m depth founded in stiff clay, avoiding vibration impacts and allowing immediate loading of the steel sub-floor.

projects.title: Truganina Distribution Warehouse Piling
projects.summary: Bored and screw piling package for a large-scale distribution warehouse slab and footings.
projects.description: Over 450 piles were installed for a 35,000m² distribution centre. Pile types included CFA piles under racking legs and screw piles under slab thickenings. Production rates were optimised using two rigs working in parallel.

projects.title: Brisbane Residential Retaining Wall Piling
projects.summary: Contiguous pile retaining wall for a split-level residential block.
projects.description: Installed a contiguous drilled pile retaining wall to support a driveway and house platform on a steeply sloping site. Works included excavation behind the wall, subsoil drainage and backfilling.

projects.title: Melbourne Rear Boundary Retaining Wall Piles
projects.summary: Steel H-pile and concrete sleeper retaining wall along a rear boundary for a townhouse site.
projects.description: To maximise usable backyard space, GroundCore installed H-piles at 1.8m centres for a concrete sleeper retaining wall up to 1.2m high, founded in stiff clay. Works were completed under strict access constraints using a compact tracked rig.

projects.title: Sydney Industrial Building Bulk Earthworks
projects.summary: Bulk earthworks and pad preparation for a new industrial warehouse.
projects.description: Bulk cut and fill operations created a level platform for a 20,000m² warehouse. GroundCore managed lime stabilisation of soft spots, controlled compaction and independent testing to achieve specified bearing pressures.

projects.title: Rail Corridor Bridge Piling Support
projects.summary: Specialised piling support within a live rail corridor for a new bridge structure.
projects.description: GroundCore provided plant, crews and temporary works design to support piling within a live rail environment. Strict rail safety and access windows dictated the methodology and sequencing.

resource_articles.title: How long do earthworks take for a 10-lot subdivision?
resource_articles.summary: Typical duration ranges for completing bulk and detailed earthworks on a 10-lot residential subdivision.
resource_articles.content:
For a typical 10-lot greenfield residential subdivision with average site conditions, bulk and detailed earthworks usually take between 3 and 5 weeks once access is available. This timeframe covers topsoil stripping, cut-to-fill, road boxing, lot trimming and primary compaction.

On straightforward sites with dry weather and good access, we often complete 10-lot packages in around 15–20 working days. Where there are complex services relocations, retaining walls or significant wet weather impacts, the program can extend towards 25 working days.

These durations assume that design is finalised, survey control is established and there are no unexpected contamination or rock conditions. For infill subdivisions or steep sites, we will provide a site-specific program after reviewing your plans and geotechnical report.
resource_articles.typical_duration_description: On a typical greenfield 10-lot residential subdivision, earthworks (topsoil strip, bulk cut-to-fill, road boxing, lot trimming and compaction) usually take 3–5 weeks, or roughly 15–25 working days, in normal weather conditions.

resource_articles.title: How to use the Pile Estimator for your project
resource_articles.summary: Step-by-step instructions for entering building dimensions, soil type and load category into the Pile Estimator.
resource_articles.content:
Our online Pile Estimator is designed for early-stage cost planning. To use it:

1. Enter the overall building length and width in metres. For example, a 30m x 20m warehouse has a footprint of 600m².
2. Select the soil type that best matches your geotechnical information (e.g. firm clay, stiff clay, sand, or rock).
3. Choose the building use or load category, such as ‘Light industrial / warehouse’, ‘Residential slab’ or ‘Heavy industrial’.
4. Click Calculate. The tool will recommend an indicative pile spacing, estimate the number of piles and apply a typical unit rate to give you a budget cost.

The results are indicative only and do not replace a project-specific design. Once you are happy with the estimate, click ‘Request Detailed Quote’ to send the inputs to our design team.

resource_articles.title: Residential screw piles vs bored piers for extensions
resource_articles.summary: Compare screw piles and bored piers for residential extensions, including depth capability and site disruption.
resource_articles.content:
When extending an existing home, the choice between screw piles and bored piers is often driven by access, vibration limits and required founding depth.

Screw piles are ideal for constrained rear-lot extensions because they can be installed with compact hydraulic heads and produce minimal spoil. They can easily achieve depths of 8–10m in typical Melbourne and Brisbane clays.

Bored piers are well suited to open, greenfield sites where larger rigs and spoil management are feasible. However, bored piers can be more disruptive near existing structures and are more sensitive to groundwater.

For two-storey extensions, screw piles typically provide a cleaner, faster solution with immediate load capacity, allowing builders to proceed with sub-floor framing as soon as pile installation is complete.

resource_articles.title: Piling rig hire: minimum hire, ratings and inclusions
resource_articles.summary: Answers to common questions about hiring piling rigs, including day rates, operator options and rating system.
resource_articles.content:
Our piling rigs are available on daily and weekly hire. Each listing on the Equipment Hire page shows the current daily rate, typical production range and an average customer rating out of 5 stars.

All rates exclude GST and mobilisation/demobilisation unless otherwise noted. Most small and mid-size rigs have a one-day minimum hire, while large CFA rigs may have a three-day minimum.

You can filter rigs by rating (e.g. 4 stars and above) and maximum daily rate to find the most cost-effective option that still meets your production requirements. Once you’ve selected a rig, click ‘Hire Now’ to add it to your booking and set the number of hire days.

resource_articles.title: What to expect from a site inspection for piling or earthworks
resource_articles.summary: Overview of the online site inspection booking tool, including how we select the nearest branch to your postcode.
resource_articles.content:
Our online booking tool makes it simple to schedule a site inspection for piling, retaining walls or earthworks.

Enter your site postcode and the system will automatically suggest the nearest GroundCore branch based on drive distance and service radius. You can then choose the relevant service type, such as ‘Retaining wall piling’ or ‘Basement excavation’, and select an available date and time.

A typical inspection takes 45–60 minutes on site. Your estimator will review access constraints, existing structures, overhead/underground services and soil information where available. Following the visit, we usually issue a written proposal within 3–5 business days, depending on complexity.
resource_articles.typical_duration_description: Most site inspections take 45–60 minutes on site, with a written proposal typically issued within 3–5 business days afterwards.

resource_articles.title: Our safety management system and ISO 45001
resource_articles.summary: How our certified ISO 45001 safety management system is applied on piling and earthworks projects.
resource_articles.content:
GroundCore operates a certified ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System across all piling, earthworks and equipment hire operations.

Key elements include:

- Formal risk assessments and Safe Work Method Statements (SWMS) for high-risk construction activities such as piling, deep excavations and plant movements.
- Pre-start briefings and toolbox talks tailored to each site.
- Competency verification for plant operators and rig crews.
- Incident reporting and investigation processes focused on root cause and corrective actions.
- Regular internal audits and external surveillance audits by an accredited certification body.

ISO 45001 certification ensures our systems are structured and continually improved, but we also work closely with clients to align with site-specific safety requirements.

resource_articles.title: Do I need engineering for retaining wall piling?
resource_articles.summary: Explains when engineered designs are required for retaining wall piles and typical approval pathways.
resource_articles.content:
In most Australian states, any retaining wall over 1m high or supporting surcharge loads (such as driveways or buildings) requires an engineered design.

For piled retaining walls, an engineer will specify pile spacing, diameter, toe depth and steel sections based on soil conditions and the height of retained ground. We can either work with your appointed engineer or recommend a trusted design partner.

Council approval requirements vary by municipality. As a rule of thumb, plan approvals are required where walls interface with property boundaries or public land. Your designer or builder should confirm local requirements early in the planning phase.

resource_articles.title: How our custom earthworks packages work
resource_articles.summary: Overview of the earthworks package configurator, machine hours and budget pricing.
resource_articles.content:
The Earthworks Packages configurator lets you bundle multiple services—such as excavation, haulage and compaction—into a single package based on estimated machine hours.

You can add line items for different machine types (e.g. 20t excavator, tandem tippers, padfoot roller) and adjust the number of hours for each. The configurator automatically totals the machine hours and calculates an indicative budget price.

For most small to medium projects, keeping total machine hours under 40 hours helps maintain cost efficiency and minimises mobilisation charges. Once you’re happy with the configuration and budget total, click ‘Request Quote for This Package’ to send the details to our estimators.

resource_articles.title: When should I request a callback instead of a quote?
resource_articles.summary: Guidance on using the callback form for early planning questions and schedule discussions.
resource_articles.content:
Use the callback request form when you are still scoping your project and need to discuss options, constraints or indicative timeframes before requesting a formal quote.

Typical reasons to request a callback include:

- You are planning a multi-lot subdivision and want to understand typical earthworks durations and staging.
- You have a complex basement or retaining wall project and need advice on constructability.
- You are unsure whether piling is required or if ground improvement would be more suitable.

In the form, briefly describe your project and select a preferred contact window (for example 9:30–10:30 AM). Our estimators aim to return calls within one business day where possible.
resource_articles.typical_duration_description: Callbacks are usually returned within one business day, during the preferred contact window you specify on the form.

resource_articles.title: Case study: Melbourne CBD basement excavation under tight program
resource_articles.summary: How we delivered a deep basement excavation in Melbourne CBD while maintaining a compressed program and strict neighbour controls.
resource_articles.content:
This case study details our approach to a CBD basement excavation with tight time and vibration constraints. Key strategies included:

- Early coordination with the structural engineer to optimise retention design.
- Staged excavation and propping to maintain stability of adjoining heritage façades.
- Night-shift spoil haulage to reduce traffic impacts.
- Real-time vibration and movement monitoring with automated alerts.

The project was completed on program, with zero recordable safety incidents and positive feedback from neighbouring property owners.

resource_articles.title: New compact piling rig added to Melbourne fleet
resource_articles.summary: Announcement of a new 10-tonne class compact piling rig available for hire in Melbourne.
resource_articles.content:
We have added a new 10-tonne class compact piling rig to our Melbourne fleet. The rig is ideal for residential screw piling, bored piers and small retaining walls with limited access, including rear-lot developments and narrow driveways.

Key features:

- Width under 2.4m for improved access.
- Telescopic mast allowing working heights under 6m where required.
- Torque head suitable for common residential screw pile sizes.

The rig is available for dry hire to approved operators or wet hire with our experienced crews.

resource_articles.title: Understanding soil type selections in the Pile Estimator
resource_articles.summary: Explains how to choose the appropriate soil type option (e.g. firm clay) when running pile estimates.
resource_articles.content:
The soil type options in our Pile Estimator—such as soft clay, firm clay, stiff clay, sand and rock—are simplified categories that represent typical design parameters.

If you have a geotechnical report, use the descriptions to select the closest matching option. For example, ‘firm clay’ in the estimator corresponds broadly to undrained shear strengths in the 25–50 kPa range.

Where no geotechnical information is available, choose the option that best reflects local experience and be conservative in early budgeting. We always recommend confirming soil parameters with proper investigation before proceeding to final design and construction.

equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 10t; Max torque: 55 kNm; Max pile depth (screw): 10m; Mast height: 6.0m; Power: 120 kW; Ideal for screw piles up to 350mm and bored piers up to 450mm.

equipment.name: 12t Compact Multi-Purpose Piling Rig
equipment.description: Versatile 12t rig for screw piling and CFA work on residential and light commercial projects in Southeast Queensland.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 12t; Max torque: 65 kNm; Max depth: 12m (screw/CFA); Mast height: 7.0m; Track width: 2.5m; Suitable for inner-suburban sites with moderate access.

equipment.name: 18t Multi-Function CFA & Screw Piling Rig
equipment.description: Mid-size piling rig for CFA piles, screw piles and displacement piles on commercial slabs and retaining walls.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 18t; Max torque: 90 kNm; CFA diameter: up to 600mm; Screw piles: up to 450mm to 14m; Mast height: 8.5m; Suited to warehouse slabs and medium retaining walls.

equipment.name: 20t Heavy CFA Piling Rig

equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 20t; Max torque: 120 kNm; CFA diameter: up to 750mm; Max depth: 22m; Ideal for large warehouse and bridge projects with generous access.

equipment.name: 8t Mini Piling Rig (Low Headroom)
equipment.description: Low-headroom mini rig suitable for basements and restricted height environments where access is extremely limited.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 8t; Max torque: 45 kNm; Working height: 4.8m; Pile types: micro-piles, small screw piles to 300mm; Best for basement underpinning and tight-access jobs.

equipment.name: 20t Excavator with GP & Batter Buckets
equipment.description: General-purpose 20-tonne excavator for bulk earthworks, basement excavation and detailed trimming.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 20t; Buckets: 600mm, 900mm, 1500mm batter; Quick hitch; GPS-ready; Ideal for subdivision earthworks and basement bulk digs.

equipment.name: 14t Excavator with Hammer Option
equipment.description: 14-tonne excavator suited to smaller earthworks, detailed excavation and rock hammering around services.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 14t; Buckets: trenching & batter; Hydraulic hammer available; Zero tail-swing option; Good for infill sites and services work.

equipment.name: 8t Excavator (Rubber Tracks)
equipment.description: Compact 8-tonne excavator with rubber tracks, ideal for tight subdivision and residential earthworks on the Gold Coast.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 8t; Zero tail swing; Rubber tracks; Full bucket set; Optional tilt hitch; Ideal for smaller cut-to-fill and retaining wall footings.

equipment.name: Tandem Axle Tipper Truck
equipment.description: Tandem tipper truck for spoil haulage and quarry product delivery to and from Melbourne sites.
equipment.specifications: Payload: 12t; Body capacity: 10m3; Ideal for subdivision haulage, basement spoil removal and quarry deliveries.

equipment.name: Truck & Dog Tipper Combination
equipment.description: High-capacity truck and dog tipper combination for bulk haulage on Queensland earthworks.
equipment.specifications: Payload: 24t; Ideal for longer haul routes from subdivision and industrial bulk digs to disposal facilities.

equipment.name: 12t Padfoot Roller
equipment.description: Smooth-drum and padfoot roller for subdivision and industrial pad compaction in Melbourne and surrounds.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 12t; Drum type: padfoot with shell kit; Vibration: high/low amplitude; Ideal for structural fill and road subgrade compaction.

equipment.name: 7t Smooth Drum Roller
equipment.description: Medium smooth drum roller for smaller pads, driveways and pavement works.
equipment.specifications: Operating weight: 7t; Smooth drum; Ideal for final trim compaction of smaller projects and access roads.

equipment.name: 30t Rough Terrain Crane
equipment.description: 30t rough terrain crane available for lifting cages, beams and site infrastructure on piling and earthworks projects.
equipment.specifications: Capacity: 30t @ 3m radius; Boom length: 30m; Outriggers; Pick-and-carry capability; Supplied with operator.

equipment.name: LED Lighting Tower
equipment.description: Mobile LED lighting tower for night works on basement excavations and warehouse pads.
equipment.specifications: 4 x 320W LED heads; Max mast height: 9m; Run time: up to 60 hours; Trailer mounted; Quiet operation suitable for residential areas.

services.name: Residential Screw Piling for Extensions (up to 10m)
services.short_description: Low-vibration screw piling for single and two-storey residential extensions with depths up to 10m.
services.full_description:
This service provides engineered screw piles for new and extended residential structures, including rear house extensions, upper-storey additions and decks.

Using compact hydraulic drive heads, we can access tight rear yards and laneways with minimal disturbance to neighbours. Screw piles are torque-monitored during installation to verify capacity and can be loaded immediately, allowing builders to proceed with steel or timber sub-floor framing as soon as piling is complete.

Typical applications include 1–2 storey extensions, garage slabs and lightweight structures. The system is suitable for foundations up to 10m deep in most Melbourne and Brisbane soils, including sandy soils overlying clays.
services.typical_applications: Two-storey rear extensions, garages, decks, lightweight residential structures on soft or variable ground.
services.typical_duration_description: Most 2–3 bedroom house extensions require 1 working day on site for screw piling, with 2–4 days for larger or complex extensions.

services.name: Industrial Screw Piling for Warehouses & Light Industrial
services.short_description: High-capacity screw piles for warehouse slabs, racking legs and light industrial buildings.
services.full_description:
Engineered screw piling solutions for warehouse slabs, racking legs, tilt-panel footings and light industrial structures. Screw piles are particularly effective where access is limited, vibration must be controlled or founding depths exceed conventional pad footings.

We design pile layouts to suit slab joints, column grids and racking layouts. Installation is carried out with mid-size rigs capable of production rates that keep pace with large floor plates.

Suitable for new industrial estates and brownfield upgrades, including projects where floor loading requirements are uncertain and future-proofing is important.
services.typical_applications: Distribution warehouses, cold stores, light industrial factories, racking legs, tilt-panel footings.
services.typical_duration_description: For a typical 30m x 20m warehouse grid, screw piling is usually completed in 2–4 working days, depending on pile numbers and access.

services.name: Residential Bored Piers for Slabs & Beams
services.short_description: Conventional bored pier foundations for new house slabs, edge beams and garages.
services.full_description:
Supply and installation of bored concrete piers for conventional residential slabs, edge beams, garages and small retaining walls. Suitable for greenfield sites and knock-down/rebuild projects with good access.

We coordinate with your builder and concrete contractor to set out, drill and clean pier holes to design depth and diameter, place reinforcement cages and concrete, and provide as-built records where required.
services.typical_applications: New house slabs, garage slabs, verandas and small retaining walls on standard lots.
services.typical_duration_description: Most standard house slab pier packages are completed in 1 working day, with larger or sloping sites requiring up to 2 days.

services.name: Basement Excavation & Retention (Urban Sites)
services.short_description: Bulk and detailed excavation, retention and dewatering for single and multi-level basements.
services.full_description:
End-to-end basement excavation services for residential and commercial projects, including bulk excavation, detailed trimming, retention systems and dewatering.

We coordinate closely with piling contractors or provide an integrated package that includes retention piles, shotcrete walls and temporary propping. Real-time monitoring and detailed staging ensure stability of adjoining properties and compliance with movement and vibration limits.

This service is suited to tight inner-urban sites where access, noise and traffic management need careful planning.
services.typical_applications: Single and multi-level basement car parks, undercroft parking, plant rooms and storage basements in urban areas.
services.typical_duration_description: A typical two-level basement for a mid-rise building is excavated over 4–8 weeks, depending on footprint, retention type and access.

services.name: Subdivision Bulk Earthworks (Greenfield 5–50 Lots)
services.short_description: Bulk cut-to-fill, road boxing and lot trim for greenfield residential subdivisions from 5 to 50 lots.
services.full_description:
Comprehensive bulk earthworks for greenfield residential subdivisions, including topsoil stripping, bulk cut-to-fill, road boxing, batters, swales and lot trimming to design levels.

We integrate survey set-out, level checks and compaction testing, and coordinate closely with service contractors and planners to maintain program and staging requirements.

Our processes align with typical land development authority standards in VIC, NSW and QLD.
services.typical_applications: Greenfield residential subdivisions between 5 and 50 lots, including local roads, building pads and drainage corridors.
services.typical_duration_description: For a typical 10-lot greenfield subdivision, bulk and detailed earthworks usually take 3–5 weeks (around 15–25 working days) in normal weather conditions.

services.name: Excavation – Bulk & Detailed
services.short_description: Bulk and detailed excavation for pads, basements, footings and service trenches.
services.full_description:
Excavation service covering both bulk earthworks and fine trimming for foundations, pads, basements and service trenches. Available as a stand-alone service or as part of a bundled earthworks package.

We match machine size to site constraints, from 8t compact excavators through to 30t bulk earthmoving units. GPS machine control can be provided where tight tolerances are required.

Ideal for custom earthworks packages where hours for excavators need to be defined up front for budget purposes.
services.typical_applications: Building pads, basement bulk digs, footing trenches, service trenches and detention basin shaping.
services.typical_duration_description: On a small 3-lot infill development, typical excavation scopes are completed over 2–4 working days, depending on access and spoil volumes.

services.name: Haulage & Truck Hire for Earthworks
services.short_description: Tandem and truck-and-dog haulage for spoil export and quarry material import.
services.full_description:
Flexible haulage service using tandem tippers and truck-and-dog combinations for spoil export, clean fill relocation and quarry material deliveries.

Available as part of a complete earthworks package or as truck-only hire, this service is commonly bundled with excavation and compaction in the Earthworks Packages configurator.

We coordinate load sequencing and traffic management to minimise delays on constrained urban streets and estate developments.
services.typical_applications: Spoil export from basements, subdivision cut-to-fill balancing, quarry deliveries for pavement layers and select fill import.
services.typical_duration_description: Haulage durations vary with distance and volume; on a 3-lot infill project, typical truck hire components run 1–2 days alongside excavation.

services.name: Compaction – Padfoot & Smooth Drum Roller Hire
services.short_description: Roller hire and compaction crews for building pads, road subgrades and car parks.
services.full_description:
Roller hire service providing padfoot and smooth drum rollers with experienced operators for structural fill, road subgrades and pavement layers.

Works can be booked as machine-only or as part of a fully managed earthworks package including excavation and haulage. Nuclear density testing can be arranged where specified.

Ideal for users of the Earthworks Packages configurator who need to allocate a defined number of compaction hours within their total machine-hours cap.
services.typical_applications: House pads, warehouse platforms, subdivision road formation and car park pavements.
services.typical_duration_description: On a small 3-lot infill development, compaction typically requires 6–10 machine hours, often spread over 1–2 days.

services.name: Retaining Wall Piling – Residential Sites
services.short_description: Drilled and screw pile retaining walls for residential boundaries, driveways and cut batters.
services.full_description:
Design and installation of piled retaining walls for residential projects, including rear boundaries, driveway cuts and benched house pads.

We offer a range of systems including steel H-pile and concrete sleeper walls, contiguous bored pile walls and screw pile walls with shotcrete facing. All solutions are engineered to suit site-specific soil conditions and surcharge requirements.

This service is often paired with a site inspection to confirm access, heights and drainage requirements prior to final design and pricing.
services.typical_applications: Rear boundary retaining walls, driveway cuts, benched pads for sloping sites and garden retaining structures over 1m high.
services.typical_duration_description: Most residential retaining wall piling jobs up to 20m length are completed over 1–3 working days, depending on access and wall height.

services.name: Site Inspection – Retaining Wall Piling
services.short_description: On-site inspection focused on retaining wall piling along boundaries and cut batters.
services.full_description:
A focused site inspection service for proposed retaining wall piling, typically along rear or side boundaries, driveway cuts or benched pads.

During the visit, our estimator reviews wall alignment, height changes, access constraints, adjoining structures and existing services. We also discuss likely pile types (e.g. H-pile and sleeper, contiguous piles) and finish options so that design and pricing can be tailored to your site.

This inspection type aligns with the 'Retaining wall piling' option in the online booking tool and is suitable for both new builds and retrofits on existing properties.
services.typical_applications: Pre-design assessment for retaining walls along rear boundaries, side boundaries and driveway cuts.
services.typical_duration_description: Most retaining wall piling inspections take 45–60 minutes on site, with a written proposal typically issued within 3–5 business days.

services.name: Site Inspection – General Piling & Earthworks
services.short_description: General on-site review of piling and earthworks requirements for residential, commercial and industrial projects.
services.full_description:
A general-purpose site inspection covering piling, earthworks and retaining wall requirements for new builds, extensions and subdivisions.

Suitable where you are still scoping the project and need advice on options, staging and likely budgets before proceeding to detailed design or formal quoting. We review access, levels, services, soil information (where available) and nearby structures.
services.typical_applications: Early-stage assessments for house sites, extensions, warehouses, subdivisions and retaining wall schemes.
services.typical_duration_description: General inspections typically take 45–60 minutes, with follow-up advice or a budget quote issued within 3–5 business days.

services.name: Safety Compliance & ISO 45001 Consultation
services.short_description: Consultation on safety management practices and ISO 45001 alignment for your project.
services.full_description:
A consultation service for clients who need to understand how our certified ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System will be applied on their project.

We can review your project-specific requirements, principal contractor systems and client WHS obligations, and explain how our risk assessments, SWMS, competency checks and audit processes integrate with your site rules.

Ideal for safety managers, principal contractors and government clients seeking assurance before contract award.
services.typical_applications: Pre-award safety reviews, client WHS briefings, alignment meetings and documentation reviews for ISO 45001-compliant projects.
services.typical_duration_description: Most safety compliance consultations are delivered as a 60–90 minute online or in-person session, with follow-up documentation provided within 2–3 business days.

service_categories.name: Piling Foundations
service_categories.description: Design and installation of screw piles, bored piers and other piling solutions for residential, commercial and industrial foundations.

service_categories.name: Earthworks & Excavation
service_categories.description: Bulk and detailed earthworks for basements, subdivisions, pads and general site preparation, including excavation, haulage and compaction.

service_categories.name: Retaining Wall Solutions
service_categories.description: Engineered retaining wall piling systems for residential and commercial boundaries, driveway cuts and benched pads.

service_categories.name: Site Inspections
service_categories.description: On-site inspections to assess piling, retaining wall and earthworks requirements before detailed design and pricing.

service_categories.name: Advisory & Other Services
service_categories.description: Specialist advisory services including safety compliance, ISO 45001 consultations and project planning support.
